headline.purpose: PR titles — the largest sans on any screen.

body.purpose: Prose: summaries, comments, guidance. Reading columns near 700px.

code.purpose: Diff content, paths, branches, stats, logs — the mono voice of git.

shadows.name: float
shadows.purpose: Only for elements floating over the page: tour bar, notification panel. In-flow elements use borders, never shadows.

motion.name: spin
motion.purpose: Loading spinners — currently the system's only animation.

components.name: Quiet Button
components.description: The default control everywhere: rests muted, wakes with a Cursor Blue border on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-quiet { border: 1px solid var(--border, #30363d); background: var(--panel, #161b22); color: var(--muted, #8b949e); border-radius: 6px; padding: 4px 10px; font-size: 12px; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-quiet:hover { border-color: var(--accent, #58a6ff); color: var(--text, #e6edf3); } .ds-btn-quiet:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: var(--accent, #58a6ff); }

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Cursor Blue fill for the single advancing action in a context (tour 'next', 'post').
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { border: 1px solid var(--accent, #58a6ff); background: var(--accent, #58a6ff); color: #fff; border-radius: 6px; padding: 4px 14px; font-size: 12px; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { filter: brightness(1.1); } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.4; cursor: default; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Transparent secondary/dismiss action; hover brings the accent border.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { border: 1px solid var(--border, #30363d); background: transparent; color: var(--muted, #8b949e); border-radius: 6px; padding: 3px 10px; font-size: 12px; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { border-color: var(--accent, #58a6ff); color: var(--text, #e6edf3); }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Bordered pill; text and border recolor together per semantic state (green approved, red changes, amber stale).
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-block; font-size: 11px; padding: 1px 8px; border-radius: 10px; border: 1px solid var(--border, #30363d); color: var(--muted, #8b949e); background: transparent; } .ds-badge-good { color: var(--green, #3fb950); border-color: var(--green, #3fb950); } .ds-badge-bad { color: var(--red, #f85149); border-color: var(--red, #f85149); }

components.name: Path Input
components.description: Mono input for path-like values; focus swaps the border to Cursor Blue.
components.css: .ds-input { padding: 10px 14px; border: 1px solid var(--border, #30363d); border-radius: 6px; background: var(--panel, #161b22); color: var(--text, #e6edf3); font-family: ui-monospace, 'SF Mono', Menlo, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 13px; outline: none; width: 100%; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: var(--accent, #58a6ff); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: var(--muted, #8b949e); }

components.name: Panel Card
components.description: The in-flow container: Graphite Panel fill, carved by a 1px border, never shadowed.
components.html:
review rating

Small, focused change with clear intent. Tests cover the new path.
components.css: .ds-card { border: 1px solid var(--border, #30363d); border-radius: 8px; background: var(--panel, #161b22); padding: 12px 16px; font-size: 13px; color: var(--text, #e6edf3); } .ds-card-title { font-weight: 600; margin-bottom: 6px; } .ds-card-body { margin: 0; line-height: 1.5; }

components.name: Segmented Modes
components.description: Mode switcher: bordered group on the raised layer; active segment gets panel fill plus a 1px ring.
components.css: .ds-modes { display: flex; gap: 2px; padding: 2px; border: 1px solid var(--border, #30363d); border-radius: 6px; background: var(--panel-2, #1c2129); } .ds-mode { flex: 1; border: none; background: transparent; color: var(--muted, #8b949e); font-family: ui-monospace, 'SF Mono', Menlo, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 10px; padding: 3px 10px; border-radius: 4px; cursor: pointer; } .ds-mode:hover { color: var(--text, #e6edf3); } .ds-mode-on { background: var(--panel, #161b22); color: var(--text, #e6edf3); box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px var(--border, #30363d); }

components.name: Tour Bar
components.description: The signature floating guide: Cursor Blue border, Float shadow, the one element allowed above the diff.
components.html:
2/7The new storeserver/utils/riskStore.ts
This introduces the persistence layer the heatmap reads from — check the eviction logic first.
← → esc
components.css: .ds-tour { border: 1px solid var(--accent, #58a6ff); border-radius: 10px; background: var(--panel, #161b22); box-shadow: 0 8px 30px rgba(0,0,0,0.5); padding: 10px 14px; font-size: 13px; color: var(--text, #e6edf3); max-width: 680px; } .ds-tour-head { display: flex; gap: 10px; align-items: baseline; } .ds-tour-step { font-family: ui-monospace, 'SF Mono', Menlo, monospace; font-size: 11px; color: var(--accent, #58a6ff); } .ds-tour-title { font-weight: 600; } .ds-tour-path { font-family: ui-monospace, 'SF Mono', Menlo, monospace; font-size: 11px; color: var(--muted, #8b949e); overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-tour-note { margin-top: 6px; color: var(--muted, #8b949e); line-height: 1.5; } .ds-tour-actions { display: flex; gap: 8px; align-items: center; margin-top: 8px; } .ds-tour-keys { margin-right: auto; color: var(--muted, #8b949e); font-size: 11px; } .ds-tour-nav { border: 1px solid var(--border, #30363d); background: transparent; color: var(--text, #e6edf3); border-radius: 6px; padding: 3px 12px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 12px; } .ds-tour-nav:hover { border-color: var(--accent, #58a6ff); } .ds-tour-primary { background: var(--accent, #58a6ff); border-color: var(--accent, #58a6ff); color: #fff; }

narrative.overview: differ is a quiet, dark study built for one job: reading code with full attention. The diff is the room; every other element — navigation, guidance, notifications — is furniture, arranged so nothing competes with the code for attention. The palette is dimmed graphite: matte, understated layers of blue-black that recede behind syntax-highlighted code, with a single Cursor Blue accent that marks wherever the reviewer's attention should go next. Warmth lives in the words (the tours, the ask-yourself prompts, the patient guidance), never in the chrome.
keyCharacteristics: Dark, flat, border-defined; surfaces are carved by 1px lines, not lifted by shadows.
Monospace is the voice of anything git-flavored: brand, paths, branches, stats, logs, labels.
One accent (Cursor Blue) marks interactivity and attention; green/red/amber are reserved semantic signals.
Small, dense, sturdy controls that rest muted and wake on interaction.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Cursor Blue is the only decorative accent, and it always means 'attention' or 'interactive'. Green, red, and amber may appear only with their semantic meaning — never as decoration.

rules.name: The Wash Rule
rules.body: Colored area fills are translucent washes over the graphite (12–30% alpha), never opaque color blocks. Tints layer via background-image so diff coloring and tour highlights stack without erasing each other.

rules.name: The Mono Voice Rule
rules.body: If it came from git or names the tool — paths, branches, +/− stats, hashes, the brand — it is monospace. If it's for a human to read as prose, it is sans. No exceptions in either direction.

rules.name: The Lowercase Rule
rules.body: The brand, buttons, and short UI copy are lowercase. Title Case is for PR titles and user content only.

rules.name: The Carved Rule
rules.body: Depth is drawn, not cast. If an element sits in the page flow, it gets a border, not a shadow. If it floats over the page, it gets the Float shadow — both, always.

dos: Do keep the diff the room: chrome stays graphite and muted, code carries all the color.
Do carve with 1px Carve Border (#30363d) lines and background steps; reserve the Float shadow for the tour bar and notification panel only.
Do put anything git-flavored in monospace and keep UI copy lowercase and terse.
Do rest controls muted and wake them with Cursor Blue on hover/focus.
Do hold WCAG AA: body text ≥4.5:1, visible focus states, reduced-motion alternatives for any animation.
Do keep reading columns near 700px while letting the diff layout span the full width.
donts: Don't ship AI-tool gimmickry: no sparkle icons, purple gradients, glassmorphism, or chat bubbles.
Don't drift into enterprise-dashboard territory: no KPI tiles, card walls, or chrome that outweighs content.
Don't read as a GitHub clone: the diff idiom is shared vocabulary, but layouts, voice, and guidance surfaces are differ's own.
Don't go SaaS-glossy or consumer-cute: no onboarding funnels, marketing sheen, bouncy motion, or illustration-heavy friendliness.
Don't use color decoratively: green/red/amber only with semantic meaning; a second decorative accent is prohibited.
Don't cast shadows on in-flow elements or use opaque color blocks where a wash belongs.
